title: 11 Settlement for CRA Business Tax Transactions
body_text:
11	Settlement for CRA Business Tax Transactions
11.1	Settlement between Central 1 and Financial Institutions in BC and Ontario
The information in this chapter is for financial institutions in BC and Ontario. Financial institutions outside of BC and Ontario settle with their provincial centrals.
Settlement for CRA business tax payments is posted to your financial institutionâs Central 1 account on the business day following receipt of the bill payment transactions at Central 1. The settlement effective date is the date Central 1 receives the payment. For payments sent to Central 1 on Saturdays, Sundays, or statutory holidays, settlement is posted on the following business day. The settlement effective date isnât backdated.
The settlement process for CRA business tax payments is the same as for Electronic Bill Payments. At 23:59:59 PT, the settlement day is closed, and any transactions received after that time are included in the following dayâs settlement. Settlement is posted to your financial institutionâs Central 1 account as code âTXâ, Bill Payment Tax Settlement. CRA business tax payments are included in this code.
11.2	Settlement between Your Financial Institution and Your Customer
Since CRA business tax transactions are guaranteed funds, itâs important to debit your customerâs account and transfer funds to an internal GL account before submitting payments to CRA. This applies to payments made in Business Taxes and to payments submitted by mail or courier.
If a cheque is accepted in payment of a GST/HST, Excise, Taxation, or Customs remittance and the cheque is dishonoured by the Drawee financial institution, attempt to recover funds. If funds arenât recovered, request reimbursement from the federal government. See the Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part for procedures.
Settlement to Your Customerâs Biller Account
CRA considers the processing date in Business Taxes as the payment date for electronic remittance vouchers. For paper remittance vouchers, CRA considers the date stamp as the payment date.
END
